lyrics:
Feel you staring, from the wall
Eyes like heat, they see it all
Hidden sparks when you move
I don't think, I like, I take

Breath is thick, head is spinning
Hands are cold, I am tripping
No words, no signs
Hit the gas, I am flying

Burn me, that shit is tight
Burn me, make me feel alright
Fire and skin, a perfect fix
Set me off, don't let me quit-Burn me

Lips say nothing, bodies speak loud
Pull me close, don't slow down
Fingers trace my skin
One more kiss, I move in

Breath is thick, head is spinning
Body's hot, I am stripping
No words, no signs
Hit the gas, I am flying

Burn me, that shit is tight
Burn me, make me feel alright
Fire and skin, a perfect fix
Set me off, don't let me quit-Burn me

Single nights, endless fails
No one hears, no one calls
Made up smiles, faceless names
The needless longing ends…

Burn me, that shit is tight
Burn me, make me feel alright
Fire and skin, a perfect fix
Set me off, don't let me quit-Burn me

Breath is thick, head is spinning
Body's spent, I am dripping
No words, no signs
Hit the gas, I am flying

Burn me, that shit is tight
Burn me, make me feel alright
Fire and skin, a perfect fix
Set me off, don't let me quit-Burn me

Burn me
Burn me
Burn me
Burn me
persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This high-energy modern rock track presents an urgent and rebellious atmosphere, built on a foundation of powerful, distorted electric guitars and a commanding female lead vocal. The production is dense and polished, creating a tight, driving feel where a wall of wide guitars provides a solid backdrop for a punchy, centered rhythm section and an upfront vocal. The mix feels full without being cluttered, allowing each instrument to occupy its own space effectively. A defining quality of the track is the explosive dynamic shift into the anthemic chorus, where the chant-like hook "Burn me" creates a memorable and powerful peak.

review.review_text: At 152 BPM in G minor and written in 3/4, 'Burn Me' moves with an urgency that shouldn't work as well as it does, a waltz-time engine driving something that feels more like a sprint. The chorus lands the way it needs to: 'fire and skin, a perfect fix' hits like a chant, and the production earns its density, with the guitars widening right on cue without burying the vocal. The bridge, 'single nights, endless fails / no one hears, no one calls,' is the quiet punch that reframes everything before the final chorus burns it down. A highlight, and one of STAB's most tightly constructed tracks.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'fire and skin, a perfect fix'
quoted bridge: 'single nights, endless fails / no one hears, no one calls'
